description: Unofficial Rust client for the LabKey Server REST API, ported from the official JS/Java clients. Typed async access to query, security, domain, experiment, assay, pipeline, report, and other modules.

userStories.title: Semver guardrails and common audit type
userStories.description: As a library maintainer, I want to apply one-time non-exhaustive and shared-type changes now so that future API growth does not cause additional breaking changes.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/client.rs` marks `ClientConfig` with `#[non_exhaustive]` and existing in-crate construction paths still compile.
`src/filter.rs` marks both `FilterType` and `ContainerFilter` with `#[non_exhaustive]`.
`src/error.rs` adds `LabkeyError::InvalidInput(String)` for client-side validation failures.
`src/common.rs` is created with `AuditBehavior` only, using `#[non_exhaustive]` and derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize`.
`src/lib.rs` exposes `pub mod common;` and does not re-export `AuditBehavior` at crate root.
`AuditBehavior` has serde round-trip tests for `NONE`, `SUMMARY`, and `DETAILED`, plus a `variant_count` regression test.
A test verifies `ClientConfig` construction still works inside this crate after the non-exhaustive change.
New/changed public items introduced in this story have rustdoc comments.
New source files added to `.gitignore` allow list.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented semver guardrails and shared audit vocabulary. Marked ClientConfig, FilterType, and ContainerFilter as #[non_exhaustive], added LabkeyError::InvalidInput(String) for client-side validation failures, and introduced src/common.rs with non-exhaustive AuditBehavior plus serde wire mappings for NONE/SUMMARY/DETAILED. Added targeted tests for AuditBehavior round-trips and variant-count regression and an explicit in-crate ClientConfig construction test after the non-exhaustive change. Added ClientConfig::new and updated rustdoc examples to keep external construction ergonomic. Exported common as a module without root re-export of AuditBehavior, allow-listed src/common.rs in .gitignore, and verified all gates with just check.

userStories.title: Client request options and multipart plumbing
userStories.description: As an SDK developer, I want configurable internal request options so that later endpoints can support timeout, redirect behavior, accepted statuses, and multipart POSTs consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/client.rs` defines `pub(crate) RequestOptions` with fields `timeout: Option<Duration>`, `no_follow_redirects: bool`, and `accepted_statuses: Vec<reqwest::StatusCode>`, deriving `Debug, Default`.

`LabkeyClient` gains `get_with_options`, `post_with_options`, and `post_multipart` internal methods that apply timeout and optional no-redirect behavior via a one-off `reqwest::Client` with `redirect::Policy::none()`.
Response handling in new option-aware methods accepts either standard success statuses or explicitly listed `accepted_statuses`, with existing error mapping behavior preserved.
Existing `get` and `post` delegate to the new option-aware methods using `RequestOptions::default()` without changing external API signatures.
`Cargo.toml` enables reqwest `multipart` feature if it is not already enabled.
Unit tests verify `RequestOptions::default()` values and confirm refactored `get`/`post` behavior remains compatible with existing URL/header expectations.
No `.unwrap()` or `.expect()` is introduced in library code.
Internal/private additions are documented where needed for maintainability; no new public undocumented API is introduced.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Added internal request option plumbing in src/client.rs by introducing pub(crate) RequestOptions with timeout, no_follow_redirects, and accepted_statuses defaults. Implemented get_with_options and post_with_options and refactored existing get/post to delegate through RequestOptions::default() to preserve external signatures. Added crate-internal post_multipart for multipart/form-data support and enabled reqwest multipart feature in Cargo.toml. Updated response handling to treat configured accepted statuses as success while preserving existing LabkeyError::Api and LabkeyError::UnexpectedResponse mapping for other non-success responses. Added unit tests for RequestOptions defaults and refactored GET/POST request construction compatibility (URL, headers, auth, timeout, and JSON content type). Verified all quality gates with just check before and after code-review feedback integration.

userStories.title: Integration test harness with mock server
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want reusable HTTP integration test infrastructure so that client behavior can be validated end-to-end for status handling, multipart, and request wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/common/mod.rs` is added with `test_client(mock_server_url: &str) -> LabkeyClient`, `fixture<T: DeserializeOwned>(name: &str) -> T`, and re-exports for shared integration test use.
`Cargo.toml` adds either `wiremock` or `mockito` under `[dev-dependencies]` and integration tests compile against it.
`tests/client_integration.rs` includes tests for GET URL/query/auth/header behavior and POST URL/body/auth behavior.
`tests/client_integration.rs` includes tests proving non-2xx JSON API errors map to `LabkeyError::Api` and non-JSON failures map to `LabkeyError::UnexpectedResponse`.
`tests/client_integration.rs` includes tests exercising `RequestOptions` timeout plumbing path and multipart request content/form-part behavior.
`tests/fixtures/` directory is created for reusable JSON payloads (empty is acceptable in this commit).
`.gitignore` is updated to allow all new tracked test files/paths for this story.
Integration tests follow project conventions (files under `tests/`, no unrelated refactors).
New source files added to `.gitignore` allow list.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented reusable integration-test infrastructure with wiremock by adding tests/common/mod.rs helpers for test client construction, fixture loading, and shared mock re-exports. Added tests/client_integration.rs coverage for GET and POST request wiring (URL, params/body shape, auth/header behavior), non-2xx error mapping (JSON API errors -> LabkeyError::Api, non-JSON failures -> LabkeyError::UnexpectedResponse), timeout request-options plumbing via internal feature-gated test support, and multipart form submission/content checks. Added tests/fixtures/api_error.json, added wiremock dev-dependency, and allow-listed all new tracked test paths in .gitignore. Refined test hooks to avoid expanding default public API surface by moving them to a feature-gated internal test-support module. Verified all quality gates with just check after implementation and after review feedback updates.

userStories.title: Query mutation endpoints core set
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want insert/update/delete/truncate query mutation endpoints so that I can perform row-level and table-level write operations through typed Rust methods.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/query.rs` adds `ModifyRowsResults` as `#[non_exhaustive]` with derives `Debug, Clone, Deserialize` and camelCase serde mapping.
`src/query.rs` adds internal `MutateRowsBody` with `#[derive(Serialize)]` and `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` on optional fields; `serde_json::json!` is not used.

`InsertRowsOptions`, `UpdateRowsOptions`, `DeleteRowsOptions`, and `TruncateTableOptions` are implemented with bon builders, derive `Debug, Clone`, and are `#[non_exhaustive]` without `Serialize`/`Deserialize` derives.

`LabkeyClient` implements `insert_rows`, `update_rows`, `delete_rows`, and `truncate_table` using shared body-shaping logic and endpoint actions `query-insertRows.api`, `query-updateRows.api`, `query-deleteRows.api`, and `query-truncateTable.api`.
Each endpoint supports optional `container_path` through its options struct.
Body contracts match plan exactly: required keys present per endpoint and optional keys omitted when `None`; `auditBehavior` serializes to expected wire values.
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.

Tests cover, for each endpoint in scope: URL construction, body construction (required/optional omissions), enum wire values, response deserialization (happy/minimal), envelope extraction behavior if applicable, and edge cases (including empty rows and zero `rowsAffected`).
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented the core query mutation endpoint set in src/query.rs by adding non-exhaustive builder options for insert_rows, update_rows, delete_rows, and truncate_table along with a non-exhaustive ModifyRowsResults response model using camelCase serde mapping. Added internal typed MutateRowsBody request shaping with skip_serializing_if on optional fields so required keys are emitted per endpoint and optional keys are omitted when unset, with AuditBehavior wired to NONE/SUMMARY/DETAILED values. Added shared internal mutate_rows transport helper and public endpoint methods with container_path override support plus rustdocs including # Errors and no_run # Example sections. Expanded tests with unit coverage for mutation URL routing, body serialization contracts (including truncate omitting rows), AuditBehavior wire values, and ModifyRowsResults happy/minimal deserialization including zero rowsAffected, plus integration coverage for each endpoint verifying URL/action, container override forwarding, strict JSON body shape, and empty rows edge behavior. Verified all quality gates with just check before and after review feedback updates.

userStories.title: Query mutation endpoints move and save

acceptanceCriteria: `src/query.rs` adds `MoveRowsOptions`, `MoveRowsResponse`, `SaveRowsOptions`, `SaveRowsCommand`, `SaveRowsResponse`, and `CommandType` in this module only.
All new option structs use bon builders, are `#[non_exhaustive]`, derive `Debug, Clone`, and do not derive serde traits.
`CommandType` is a C-like enum with derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`, is `#[non_exhaustive]`, has explicit serde wire mapping, and remains in `query.rs`.
`LabkeyClient` implements `move_rows` and `save_rows` as JSON POST endpoints with correct query controller actions.
Response types are `#[non_exhaustive]`, derive `Debug, Clone, Deserialize`, and use camelCase serde conventions.
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.

Tests cover URL construction for both endpoints, `SaveRowsCommand` serialization including command wire format, `CommandType` serde round-trip for every variant plus `variant_count`, response deserialization for move/save payloads, and edge case of empty `commands`.
No `.unwrap()` or `.expect()` is introduced in library code.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented move/save query mutation support in src/query.rs with new public types MoveRowsOptions, MoveRowsResponse, SaveRowsOptions, SaveRowsCommand, SaveRowsResponse, CommandType, and SaveRowsApiVersion following story conventions (bon builders for options, non-exhaustive response models, explicit command wire mappings). Added LabkeyClient::move_rows and LabkeyClient::save_rows using typed JSON bodies and correct controller actions query-moveRows.api and query-saveRows.api, including optional container override handling and strict omission of optional fields when unset. Added rustdocs with # Errors and no_run # Example sections for both endpoints. Expanded unit coverage for move/save endpoint URL routing, SaveRowsCommand serialization contract, CommandType exact wire value serialize/deserialize behavior plus unknown-value rejection and variant-count regression, MoveRowsResponse happy/minimal deserialization, and SaveRowsResponse empty-commands deserialization. Expanded integration coverage in tests/client_integration.rs for move_rows request body/auth/header/path behavior, save_rows empty commands edge behavior, and save_rows command wire serialization behavior. Solicited and addressed feedback from nick-isms/testing-guru/semver-nag and re-ran just check successfully before and after feedback changes.

`QueryDetailsColumn` includes `#[serde(flatten)] pub extra: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>`.
`LabkeyClient` implements `select_distinct_rows` and `get_query_details` with correct controller/action and GET param construction via existing declarative option-param patterns.
`select_distinct_rows` maps `max_rows: -1` to query parameter `showRows=all`.
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.

New source files added to `.gitignore` allow list.

userStories.notes: Implemented select-distinct and query-details read support in src/query.rs by adding SelectDistinctOptions/SelectDistinctResponse and GetQueryDetailsOptions/QueryDetailsResponse with the required supporting vocabulary types (QueryDetailsColumn, QueryLookup, QueryView, QueryViewColumn, QueryViewFilter, QueryViewSort, QueryImportTemplate, QueryIndex). Added QueryDetailsColumn #[serde(flatten)] extra for unknown fields and modeled response structs as #[non_exhaustive] with camelCase serde mapping. Implemented LabkeyClient::select_distinct_rows and LabkeyClient::get_query_details using query-selectDistinct.api and query-getQueryDetails.api, including container override support, declarative optional parameter shaping, repeated fields/viewName parameter handling, and max_rows = -1 mapping to query.showRows=all. Added tests/fixtures/query_details.json and expanded unit/integration coverage for endpoint URL and request params, select-distinct deserialization, full fixture deserialization for query details, QueryDetailsColumn optional-field present/absent behavior, top-level extra-field flatten behavior, custom dataRegionName handling, and maxRows/showRows edge behavior. Added explicit .gitignore allow-list entry for the new fixture, solicited feedback from nick-isms/testing-guru/semver-nag, incorporated test-rigor improvements, and verified all quality gates with just check before and after feedback updates.

userStories.title: Query schema and query listing endpoints
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want schema and query listing methods so that I can enumerate available query surfaces before issuing read/write operations.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/query.rs` adds `GetQueriesOptions`, `GetQueriesResponse`, `QueryInfo`, and `GetSchemasOptions` with required derive and non-exhaustive conventions.
Option structs use bon builders, are `#[non_exhaustive]`, derive `Debug, Clone`, and do not derive serde traits.
Response structs are `#[non_exhaustive]`, derive `Debug, Clone, Deserialize`, and use camelCase serde mapping where applicable.
`LabkeyClient` adds `get_queries` and `get_schemas` methods using actions `query-getQueries.api` and `query-getSchemas.api`.
`get_queries` parameter names match server contract exactly: `includeColumns`, `includeSystemQueries`, `includeTitle`, `includeUserQueries`, `includeViewDataUrl`, and `queryDetailColumns`.
`get_schemas` return type is `serde_json::Value` (the server returns a JSON object keyed by schema name).
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.

Tests cover URL construction for both endpoints, parameter construction for `get_queries` verifying all six named param keys, `get_schemas` deserialization of a representative fixture (JSON object keyed by schema name), and `GetQueriesResponse` deserialization with nested `queries` arrays.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented query listing and schema listing support in src/query.rs by adding GetQueriesOptions, GetSchemasOptions, QueryInfo, and GetQueriesResponse with non-exhaustive conventions and typed rustdocs. Added LabkeyClient::get_queries and LabkeyClient::get_schemas using query-getQueries.api and query-getSchemas.api, with exact get_queries parameter keys includeColumns/includeSystemQueries/includeTitle/includeUserQueries/includeViewDataUrl/queryDetailColumns and raw serde_json::Value return for get_schemas to preserve keyed server response shape. Added unit tests for get_queries nested queries deserialization, get_schemas fixture object deserialization, and query endpoint URL routing for both actions. Added tests/fixtures/get_schemas.json and allow-listed it in .gitignore. userStories.title: Query view management endpoints
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want query-view management methods so that I can fetch, save, session-save, and delete views with correct LabKey wire semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/query.rs` adds `GetQueryViewsOptions`, `SaveQueryViewsOptions`, `SaveSessionViewOptions`, and `DeleteQueryViewOptions` following option-struct conventions (bon builder, `#[non_exhaustive]`, `Debug, Clone`, no serde derives).
`LabkeyClient` implements `get_query_views`, `save_query_views`, `save_session_view`, and `delete_query_view` with correct controller/action routes.
`save_session_view` JSON body uses literal keys `query.queryName` and `query.viewName` (flat key names, not nested objects).
`delete_query_view` sends `complete: !revert` only when `revert` is explicitly provided; omits `complete` when `revert` is `None`.
`save_query_views` body sends boolean flags only when true, omitting false/None fields per API contract.
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.

Tests cover URL construction for all four endpoints, special JSON key semantics for `save_session_view`, `complete` logic for `delete_query_view`, and conditional boolean emission for `save_query_views`.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented query view management support in src/query.rs by adding GetQueryViewsOptions, SaveQueryViewsOptions, SaveSessionViewOptions, and DeleteQueryViewOptions as non-exhaustive bon-builder option structs. Added LabkeyClient::get_query_views, save_query_views, save_session_view, and delete_query_view with controller/action routes query-getQueryViews.api, query-saveQueryViews.api, query-saveSessionView.api, and query-deleteView.api. Implemented typed body shaping for view mutations to preserve LabKey wire semantics: save_session_view emits flat keys query.queryName/query.viewName, delete_query_view emits complete only when revert is provided (complete = !revert), and save_query_views emits shared/session/hidden only when true while omitting false/None. Added rustdocs with # Errors and no_run # Example sections for all four endpoints. Expanded unit coverage for endpoint URL routing, save-session dot-key serialization, delete complete/revert omission logic, and save-query-views flag/metadata serialization; expanded integration coverage for request URL/body contracts and edge semantics for all four endpoints. Solicited feedback from nick-isms/testing-guru/semver-nag, incorporated follow-up fixes (metadata forwarding and full true-flag coverage), and verified all quality gates with just check before and after feedback updates.

userStories.title: Query miscellaneous endpoints and SQL literal utilities
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want remaining core query utilities and helper methods so that I can validate metadata, fetch data views/server date, and safely build SQL literals.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/query.rs` adds `GetDataViewsOptions`, `DataViewType`, `ValidateQueryOptions`, `ValidateQueryResponse`, `GetServerDateResponse`, utility functions `sql_date_literal`, `sql_date_time_literal`, `sql_string_literal`, and constant `URL_COLUMN_PREFIX`.
`DataViewType` is a C-like enum with derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`, is `#[non_exhaustive]`, and includes serde wire mapping with full round-trip coverage and `variant_count` test.
Response types follow server-response conventions (`#[non_exhaustive]`, `Debug, Clone, Deserialize`, camelCase where applicable).

`LabkeyClient` implements `get_data_views`, `get_server_date`, and `validate_query` with required special behavior: `get_data_views` sends `includeData: true` and `includeMetadata: false` and extracts `response.data`; `validate_query` switches to `query-validateQueryMetadata.api` when requested; `get_server_date` uses no params and no container path.
Envelope extraction failures return `LabkeyError::UnexpectedResponse`.
SQL literal utilities double single quotes (`'` to `''`) and tests assert escaping and formatting.
Endpoint rustdocs include `# Errors` and `# Example` sections with `no_run` examples.
Tests cover URL/action switching, body contracts, envelope extraction happy/error cases, SQL literal behavior, enum wire values, `variant_count`, and `URL_COLUMN_PREFIX` regression.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Implemented US-009 query utility coverage in src/query.rs by adding URL_COLUMN_PREFIX and SQL literal helpers sql_string_literal, sql_date_literal, and sql_date_time_literal with quote escaping behavior and regression tests. Added typed query utility API surface with non-exhaustive DataViewType, GetDataViewsOptions, ValidateQueryOptions, ValidateQueryResponse, and GetServerDateResponse. Implemented LabkeyClient::get_data_views using reports-browseData.api with strict includeData=true/includeMetadata=false request contract and explicit envelope extraction of response.data, returning LabkeyError::UnexpectedResponse when data is missing or malformed. Implemented LabkeyClient::validate_query with action switching between query-validateQuery.api and query-validateQueryMetadata.api based on validate_query_metadata. Implemented LabkeyClient::get_server_date targeting query-getServerDate.api with no container path segment and no query params. Expanded unit and integration tests for endpoint URL/action routing, request body/param contracts, DataViewType serde round-trips plus unknown-value rejection and variant-count regression, envelope extraction success/error paths, SQL literal escaping/formatting behavior, URL_COLUMN_PREFIX regression, and get_server_date path semantics. Solicited feedback from nick-isms/testing-guru/semver-nag, incorporated follow-up improvements, and verified all quality gates with just check before and after feedback changes.

userStories.title: Query import_data multipart endpoint

Optional multipart fields (`format`, `insertOption`, `useAsync`, `saveToPipeline`, `importIdentity`, `importLookupByAlternateKey`) are included only when provided.
`ImportDataResponse` deserializes correctly with and without `job_id`.

userStories.notes: Implemented typed query import_data multipart support in src/query.rs with new ImportDataOptions, ImportDataSource, InsertOption, and ImportDataResponse using required non-exhaustive and derive conventions. Added LabkeyClient::import_data targeting query-import.api via post_multipart with required schemaName/queryName fields and source-specific multipart wiring for text, file bytes, path, and module-resource modes (including module + moduleResource pair semantics). Added optional multipart fields with omission-by-default behavior for format, insertOption, useAsync, saveToPipeline, importIdentity, importLookupByAlternateKey, plus auditUserComment/auditDetails passthrough for parity with upstream clients. Added InsertOption serde wire round-trip and variant-count regression tests, ImportDataResponse deserialization tests with and without jobId, import URL regression coverage, and invalid MIME-type InvalidInput coverage. Expanded integration tests to verify multipart construction for all ImportDataSource variants, required field presence, insertOption MERGE wire value, optional field wiring, and endpoint-specific error mapping (JSON API errors and non-JSON unexpected responses). Solicited feedback from nick-isms/testing-guru/semver-nag, addressed review items, and verified all gates with just check before and after feedback changes.

userStories.title: Query get_data endpoint

userStories.notes: Implemented typed query get_data support in src/query.rs with new public get-data vocabulary: GetDataOptions, GetDataSource, GetDataSourceType, GetDataSortDirection, GetDataSort, GetDataTransform, GetDataFilter, GetDataAggregate, and GetDataPivot. Added LabkeyClient::get_data targeting query-getData (no .api suffix) and returning SelectRowsResponse. userStories.title: Review iteration A: query parity audit gate
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a focused parity audit against upstream query clients so that we can detect and correct high-risk mismatches before expanding scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit scope is limited to cross-referencing upstream JS (`src/labkey/query/Utils.ts`, `src/labkey/query/GetData.ts`, `src/labkey/Query.ts`) and Java (`src/org/labkey/remoteapi/query/`) query surfaces against current Rust query implementation.

A brief endpoint-by-endpoint parity checklist is produced with explicit status per endpoint (match, partial, missing, behavior mismatch) and concrete evidence for each discrepancy. The checklist may be recorded in story notes or a tracked project file — a repo-tracked artifact is not required.
Only discrepancy reporting and minimal critical fixes are allowed; no new endpoints, broad refactors, or opportunistic feature additions are introduced.
If more than 5 discrepancies are found, all are documented but code fixes are limited to the most critical subset, with rationale recorded for deferred items.
Any code changes made during this audit include tests covering the corrected behavior and preserve existing API conventions/documentation requirements.
If new source files are created for fixes, they are added to `.gitignore` allow list.
`just check` passes (fmt, clippy with warnings denied, all tests, doc build)
userStories.notes: Completed focused query parity audit against upstream JS (Query.ts, query/Utils.ts, query/GetData.ts, query/Rows.ts, query/SelectRows.ts, query/SelectDistinctRows.ts, query/ExecuteSql.ts, query/GetQueryDetails.ts) and Java (remoteapi/query). Endpoint checklist: match = get_query_details, get_queries, get_schemas, get_query_views, save_query_views, save_session_view, delete_query_view, get_data_views, validate_query, get_server_date, insert_rows, update_rows, delete_rows, truncate_table, move_rows, save_rows, import_data; partial = select_rows (hardcoded apiVersion 17.1 and no method/timeout toggles compared to JS options), execute_sql (hardcoded apiVersion 17.1 and no requiredVersion/timeout toggles), get_data (typed modeling intentionally removes JS callback/legacy coercion behavior); missing/deferred (no code added per scope guardrail) = JS Visualization namespace, JS experimental SQL namespace, JS callback/helper exports and legacy argument-overload APIs. More than five discrepancies were documented; code fixes were limited to one high-risk wire mismatch: select_distinct_rows now emits ignoreFilter=true (matching upstream SelectDistinctRows.ts) rather than 1.
